text.title: Paid Acquisition Growth Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the growth-paid-acquisition methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: CAC ceiling MUST be computed from LTV × gross margin × target payback share. Spending above ceiling is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without ceiling, scale destroys unit economics quietly.

rule.statement: Each channel MUST pass an audience-fit gate (LinkedIn for B2B Director+; Meta for B2C+SMB; Google Search for high-intent; X for tech/B2B niche).
rule.rationale: Wrong-channel spend rarely recovers regardless of optimization.

rule.statement: Channel scaling MUST be ≤+20% per week while CAC stable; faster ramps break learning phase.
rule.rationale: +20%/wk is the empirical floor for stable Meta/Google delivery.

rule.statement: Before crossing a scaling threshold, channel MUST show 14 days of CAC under ceiling with no learning-phase reset.
rule.rationale: Variance fools you into scaling losing channels.

rule.statement: Plan MUST cite payback-window target (months) and stop-loss CAC. Both are non-negotiable triggers.
rule.rationale: Without explicit stop-loss, sunk-cost effect drives over-spend.
